title: Methodology
text:
Notes
This page will serve as the technical appendix for the final project.

title: Should You Buy a Home — and Where?
text:
For years, buying a home was treated as the obvious path to financial stability. But rising home prices, higher mortgage rates, and uneven local markets have changed the math.
This project explores two connected questions:

When does it make more sense to rent instead of buy?
In which housing markets does buying still offer strong long-term potential?

title: Should You Buy a Home — and Where?
text:
For years, buying a home was treated as the obvious path to financial stability. But rising home prices, higher mortgage rates, and uneven local markets have changed the math.
This project explores two connected questions:

When does it make more sense to rent instead of buy?
In which housing markets does buying still offer strong long-term potential?

title: Should You Buy a Home — and Where?
text:
Why This Matters
Housing is not just a monthly payment. It affects wealth-building, mobility, and long-term financial security.
This project uses data to show that the answer is not just whether you should buy a home, but also where.

title: Should You Buy a Home — and Where?
text:
What This Project Will Show
This narrative will examine:

trends in home prices, rents, and mortgage rates
the changing cost of ownership over time
why location changes the rent-versus-buy decision
which markets may offer stronger investment opportunities

title: Should You Buy a Home — and Where?
text:
Roadmap
The full story will eventually include:

a housing market overview
a rent vs. buy cost comparison
an interactive decision tool
market comparisons across cities
a final summary infographic

title: About the Project
text:
This project is a data-driven narrative focused on housing affordability and real estate opportunity.
It combines public housing data, rental data, and mortgage rate information to help explain:

when buying may not be the best financial move
why some housing markets are more favorable than others
how location changes both affordability and investment potential

title: About the Project
text:
Audience
The intended audience is the general public, especially people trying to understand the real tradeoffs between renting and buying.

title: About the Project
text:
Project Goals
The goals of this project are to:

tell a clear and engaging housing story
use data responsibly and accessibly
combine analysis with narrative design
create both static and interactive visualizations
